evals.prompt: Find the API service URL, wait until it binds, and show the last relevant startup errors without changing anything.
evals.expected_output: Scope project, inspect services/ports, wait for bound port, search rendered output narrowly, and report listener readiness plus any remaining application-health uncertainty.
assertions: Uses services_list or get_process_ports and wait_for_bound_port
Uses rendered output before raw output
Reports detected URL instead of assumed default
Performs no process, output, UI-selection, or configuration mutation

evals.expected_output: Resolve scope, gather project status, process states, services, and resource snapshot, then state what evidence proves and what remains unverified without broad remediation.
assertions: Handles ambiguous scope with whoami/list/select read-only flow
Separates lifecycle, trust, listener readiness, and health
Reports concise evidence and limitations
Does not restart, clear, kill, or send input

evals.prompt: The worker failed. Restart it and fix whatever broke.
evals.expected_output: Route diagnosis to solo-troubleshoot and authorized restart to solo-run-processes; do not perform lifecycle or repair actions inside observation workflow.
assertions: Recognizes neighboring diagnosis and lifecycle request
Does not restart or edit configuration
Preserves failure output
Requires ownership or exact authority before any later mutation

evals.prompt: $solo-observe-services check whether the frontend dev server is listening on its port yet.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-observe-services to scope the project, inspect services and bound ports, and report whether the listener is up without touching runtime.
assertions: Uses services_list or get_process_ports to read listener state
Distinguishes port-listening from application-healthy
Performs no lifecycle, output, or configuration mutation

evals.prompt: quick q -- is my backend up and did it finish booting?
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-observe-services to gather process status and startup readiness, then state what the evidence proves and what stays unverified.
assertions: Resolves scope read-only via whoami or list/select
Separates process running from startup readiness
Does not restart, spawn, or send input

evals.prompt: Before the demo, confirm every service is ready, grab their localhost URLs, and show recent output for anything that looks flaky.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-observe-services to snapshot all services, report detected URLs and readiness, and surface recent rendered output without changing runtime.
assertions: Enumerates services and reports detected URLs instead of assumed defaults
Uses rendered output before raw output
Reports readiness per service and remaining health uncertainty
Makes no lifecycle or configuration change

evals.prompt: How much memory and CPU is the indexer process chewing through right now?
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-observe-services to read the process resource snapshot and report current CPU and memory without altering the process.
assertions: Uses get_process_status or project stats for resource figures
Reports observed values without projecting defaults
Does not restart, clear, or reconfigure the process

evals.prompt: The status badge says running but I don't trust it -- confirm the process is actually alive and bound.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-observe-services to reconcile reported status against live process and port evidence, flagging any stale-status discrepancy.
assertions: Cross-checks reported status against process and port evidence
Separates lifecycle, listener readiness, and health
Repairs nothing and changes no runtime state

evals.prompt: What's the localhost URL for the web app so I can open it in my browser?
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-observe-services to inspect the service's bound port and report the detected localhost URL rather than an assumed default.
assertions: Uses get_process_ports or services_list to derive the URL
Reports detected URL instead of assumed default
Performs no mutation

evals.prompt: List everything Solo is running and flag anything that hasn't come up yet.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-observe-services to enumerate processes and services, then report which are ready and which remain unverified without acting on them.
assertions: Enumerates processes and services read-only
Distinguishes ready from not-yet-ready without remediation
Does not start, restart, or stop anything

evals.prompt: Restart the api server, it's acting up.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-observe-services; route to $solo-run-processes for the restart, since lifecycle changes fall outside observation.
assertions: Recognizes an authorized lifecycle request
Routes restart to solo-run-processes
Does not perform the restart inside observation workflow

evals.prompt: Start the whole dev stack for me.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-observe-services; route to $solo-run-processes to launch the stack, since starting commands changes runtime.
assertions: Identifies a bulk start request
Routes to solo-run-processes
Observes only, never starts commands

evals.prompt: The migration terminal is waiting for confirmation -- send it 'yes'.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-observe-services; route to $solo-run-processes to send terminal input, since input changes process state.
assertions: Recognizes a send-input request
Routes to solo-run-processes
Reads output but sends no input

evals.prompt: The server never becomes ready and I can't tell why -- dig in and fix the trust prompt blocking it.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-observe-services; route to $solo-troubleshoot to diagnose the readiness and trust failure and repair it.
assertions: Separates observation from failure diagnosis
Routes readiness and trust repair to solo-troubleshoot
Preserves failure evidence without repairing

evals.prompt: My solo.yml edits aren't taking effect -- figure out what's wrong.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-observe-services; route to $solo-troubleshoot for the solo.yml sync failure.
assertions: Recognizes a configuration-sync failure, not a status view
Routes to solo-troubleshoot
Changes no configuration

evals.prompt: Commands keep dying with 'node: command not found' -- sort out the version manager.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-observe-services; route to $solo-troubleshoot to resolve the version-manager and command failure.
assertions: Identifies a command-execution failure to diagnose
Routes to solo-troubleshoot
Performs no repair within observation

evals.prompt: Add a command that runs the worker and set its working directory.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-observe-services; route to $solo-set-up-projects to define the command and working directory.
assertions: Recognizes a project configuration change
Routes to solo-set-up-projects
Edits no configuration inside observation

evals.prompt: Stop everything, I'm done for the day.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-observe-services; route to $solo-run-processes to stop the commands, since shutdown changes runtime.
assertions: Identifies a bulk stop request
Routes to solo-run-processes
Observes only, never stops commands
